proposed_hypotheses.text: At least one feature in the dataset is associated with the binary outcome objective_response at a level beyond chance (i.e., univariate screen will surface non-null signals).

analyses.code: Univariate screen of every feature against objective_response: 2x2 chi-square for binary features, Spearman rank correlation for ordinal/integer features, Mann-Whitney U for continuous features, and chi-square for categorical features.
analyses.result_summary: Univariate screen identified 9 features with p<1e-3: feature_051 (ordinal, 3 levels) p=6.5e-95, feature_013 (binary) p=1.1e-33, feature_011 (cont) p=1.4e-32, feature_067 (binary) p=2.0e-18, feature_006 (binary) p=1.0e-6, feature_063 (cont) p=2.0e-6, feature_099 (cont) p=3.5e-5, feature_007 (binary) p=5.9e-4, feature_039 (binary) p=3.4e-3.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 is a 3-level ordinal feature (likely stage or ECOG performance status) with monotonically decreasing objective_response from level 0 to level 2.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_051')['objective_response'].mean(); statsmodels logit(objective_response ~ C(feature_051)).
analyses.result_summary: Response rate falls monotonically: level 0 = 0.211 (n=17557), level 1 = 0.157 (n=24962), level 2 = 0.111 (n=7481). Logit coefficients vs level 0: level 1 = -0.361 (p<1e-44), level 2 = -0.765 (p<1e-75). Strong dose-response consistent with stage/ECOG.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_011 (continuous, 0-28, mean 3.8) are associated with lower objective_response, consistent with a tumor- or comorbidity-burden marker.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_011); group means by response.
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_011 is 3.38 in responders vs 3.93 in non-responders. Logit coef = -0.0385 per unit (p<1e-31), OR=0.962. Consistent negative effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 (binary, 65% prevalence) is associated with lower objective_response, an adverse clinical or treatment-context indicator.

analyses.code: Crosstab + chi-square; multivariable logit adjusting for stage, feature_011, feature_067, feature_006, feature_039.
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 0.154 (f013=1, n=32365) vs 0.196 (f013=0, n=17635), absolute difference -0.042. Adjusted OR=0.74 (p=1.1e-34). Effect is consistent across smoking strata (rate diffs -0.043 former, -0.046 current, -0.034 never).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (binary, 25% prevalence) is independently associated with lower objective_response across stage strata, consistent with an adverse organ-involvement marker (e.g., liver metastasis).

analyses.code: Stratified rates by feature_051; multivariable logit adjusting for stage.
analyses.result_summary: Within each stage level the response rate is lower for f067=1: stage 0 0.181 vs 0.221, stage 1 0.132 vs 0.165, stage 2 0.090 vs 0.117. Stage-adjusted logit coef = -0.258 (p=4.6e-19), OR=0.77.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 (binary, 28% prevalence) is positively associated with objective_response and is more prevalent in current/former smokers than never-smokers, consistent with a smoking-related driver mutation profile.

analyses.code: Crosstab + groupby smoking; logit adjusting for stage and feature_011.
analyses.result_summary: Response 0.182 (f006=1, n=14258) vs 0.164 (f006=0, n=35742). Prevalence by smoking: current 45.0%, former 25.2%, never 7.6%. Adjusted main-effect coef = 0.13 (p=2e-6).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_039 (binary, 50/50 split) is a treatment-indicator-like feature with a small overall positive main effect on objective_response (consistent with a randomized therapy assignment whose benefit depends on biomarker subgroups).

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_039) and stage-adjusted logit.
analyses.result_summary: Response 0.174 (f039=1, n=25008) vs 0.164 (f039=0, n=24992); absolute diff +0.0099. Stage-adjusted coef = 0.076 (p=0.0017), OR=1.08. Modest main effect, balanced 50/50 across demographics — consistent with a randomized treatment assignment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_092 (continuous, 0-0.798, distribution suggesting a tumor expression fraction such as PD-L1 TPS) modifies the effect of feature_039 such that the treatment benefit is concentrated in patients with high feature_092.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_039 * feature_092 + C(feature_051) + feature_011).
analyses.result_summary: Highly significant positive interaction: feature_039:feature_092 coef = 0.607 (p=2e-7). Within feature_039=1 patients f092 coef=0.473 (p=2e-9); within feature_039=0 patients f092 coef=-0.135 (p=0.10) — classic 'predictive' (not just prognostic) biomarker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: When feature_092 is split into tertiles, treatment effect of feature_039 (rate difference) is small or null in low/middle tertiles and substantially positive in the top tertile.

analyses.code: qcut feature_092 into 3 tertiles; compute rate(f039=1) - rate(f039=0) within each tertile.
analyses.result_summary: Treatment effect by tertile (rate diff f039=1 minus f039=0): T1 +0.003 (n~16667), T2 -0.009, T3 +0.036. Effect concentrated in top tertile, consistent with a high-expression-cutoff predictive biomarker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 modifies the effect of feature_039 such that feature_006-positive patients derive a larger absolute response benefit from feature_039 than feature_006-negative patients.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_039 * feature_006 + C(feature_051) + feature_011) and 2x2x2 stratification.
analyses.result_summary: feature_039:feature_006 interaction coef = 0.179 (p=0.001). Stratified rates: f006=0/f039=0 0.163, f006=0/f039=1 0.165 (no benefit); f006=1/f039=0 0.167, f006=1/f039=1 0.197 (+3.0% benefit). f006 confers benefit only with treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_007 (binary, 45% prevalence, balanced across smoking/histology/race) modifies feature_039 effect with positive interaction (i.e., feature_007=1 patients benefit more).

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_039 * feature_007 + C(feature_051) + feature_011).
analyses.result_summary: feature_039:feature_007 interaction coef = 0.146 (p=0.0024). Stratified rates: f007=0 group has +0.0004 treatment effect (no benefit); f007=1 group has +0.0215 treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 (binary, 15% prevalence) is a NEGATIVE predictive marker — feature_027=1 patients receive no benefit (or harm) from feature_039 compared to feature_027=0 patients.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_039 * feature_027 + C(feature_051) + feature_011).
analyses.result_summary: feature_039:feature_027 interaction coef = -0.207 (p=0.0023). Stratified rates: f027=0 group rate(f039=1)=0.177 vs rate(f039=0)=0.162 (+0.014 benefit); f027=1 group rate(f039=1)=0.158 vs rate(f039=0)=0.173 (-0.015 — possible harm).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_066 (continuous, mean 3.5, range 0.29-30.4) modifies feature_039 effect with positive interaction (higher feature_066 values associated with greater benefit).

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_039 * feature_066 + C(feature_051) + feature_011).
analyses.result_summary: feature_039:feature_066 interaction coef = 0.032 per unit (p=0.005). Modest but statistically significant; higher feature_066 values shift the treatment effect upward.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment effect of feature_039 does NOT vary by stage (feature_051): the absolute and relative benefits are similar at stages 0, 1, and 2.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_039 * C(feature_051) + feature_011).
analyses.result_summary: Stage 1 vs 0 interaction coef = -0.027 (p=0.60); stage 2 vs 0 interaction coef = -0.087 (p=0.29). No statistically significant heterogeneity by stage.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment effect of feature_039 does NOT vary by smoking status (feature_057): current, former, and never smokers gain similar response benefit.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_039 * C(feature_057) + C(feature_051) + feature_011) and stratified rate diffs.
analyses.result_summary: Interaction terms (vs 'current' reference): former -0.007 (p=0.90), never -0.078 (p=0.31). Stratified rate diffs: current +0.012, former +0.011, never +0.001. No significant heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment effect of feature_039 does NOT vary by histology (feature_043): adenocarcinoma and squamous patients gain similar absolute benefit.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_039 * C(feature_043) + C(feature_051) + feature_011).
analyses.result_summary: feature_039:squamous interaction coef = 0.033 (p=0.53). Stratified rate diffs: adenocarcinoma +0.009, squamous +0.013. No meaningful heterogeneity by histology.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment effect of feature_039 does NOT vary by race (feature_123): white, black, asian, hispanic, and other patients gain similar response benefit.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_039 * C(feature_123) + C(feature_051) + feature_011); stratified rate diffs.
analyses.result_summary: Race-by-treatment interactions all p>0.05 (closest: 'other' p=0.061 with n~1014). Stratified rate diffs: white +0.009, black +0.016, asian +0.020, hispanic +0.010, other -0.030 (small subgroup). No statistically significant heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment effect of feature_039 does NOT vary by age (feature_078): older and younger patients gain similar response benefit.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_039 * feature_078 + C(feature_051) + feature_011); rate diffs by age group.
analyses.result_summary: Age x f039 interaction coef = -0.0007 per year (p=0.78). Stratified rate diffs: <55 +0.006, 55-65 +0.014, 65-75 +0.010, 75+ +0.005. No significant heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 (the strong adverse main-effect feature) does NOT significantly modify the effect of feature_039 — it is a prognostic, not a predictive, marker.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_013 * feature_039 + C(feature_051) + feature_011 + feature_067).
analyses.result_summary: feature_013:feature_039 interaction coef = 0.071 (p=0.144). Main effect of feature_013 remains strongly negative (OR=0.71). Consistent with feature_013 being a prognostic factor (e.g., comorbidity / treatment-line context) rather than a treatment-effect modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (the second strong adverse main-effect feature) does NOT significantly modify the effect of feature_039 — like feature_013 it is purely prognostic.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_067 * feature_039 + C(feature_051) + feature_011).
analyses.result_summary: feature_067:feature_039 interaction coef = -0.027 (p=0.64). Main effect of feature_067 negative (coef=-0.245). Treatment benefit similar within f067=0 and f067=1 strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A composite biomarker (feature_006=1 OR feature_092>=0.5) defines a 'biomarker-positive' subgroup (~44% of cohort) within which the feature_039 treatment effect is positive and significant, while the biomarker-negative subgroup gains essentially no benefit.

analyses.code: df['biomarker_pos'] = ((df['feature_006']==1) | (df['feature_092']>=0.5)).astype(int); logit(objective_response ~ feature_039 * biomarker_pos + C(feature_051) + feature_011).
analyses.result_summary: biomarker_pos prevalence = 43.7%. Interaction coef = 0.191 (p<1e-4). Stratified rates: biomarker-neg f039=0 0.165 vs f039=1 0.162 (-0.002, no benefit); biomarker-pos f039=0 0.163 vs f039=1 0.189 (+0.025 benefit). Composite biomarker isolates the responsive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment access (likelihood of receiving feature_039 = 1) is NOT differentially distributed by insurance, race, or age — consistent with a randomized-like assignment in this cohort.

analyses.code: logit(feature_039 ~ C(feature_005) + C(feature_123) + feature_078 + C(feature_057) + C(feature_051)).
analyses.result_summary: All covariate p-values > 0.05 (closest: uninsured p=0.065, stage 2 p=0.090). f039 prevalence varies <2% across insurance, race, and stage groups. No meaningful access disparity in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for stage, feature_011, feature_039, feature_013, and feature_067, race (feature_123) does not have a significant residual association with objective_response — i.e., no unexplained racial disparity in this dataset.

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ C(feature_123) + C(feature_051) + feature_011 + feature_039 + feature_013 + feature_067).
analyses.result_summary: All race indicator p-values > 0.12 (most ~0.5-0.97). Largest coefficient: white = -0.078 (p=0.12) vs reference (asian). No residual race disparity after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance type (feature_005) does not have a significant residual association with objective_response after adjustment — no insurance disparity in response in this cohort.

analyses.code: Univariate chi-square on feature_005 vs objective_response, plus rate inspection by insurance.
analyses.result_summary: Univariate chi-square p=0.23. Rates: medicaid 0.161, medicare 0.168, private 0.172, uninsured 0.170 — modest spread (1.1 percentage points), not statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A joint multivariable logistic model can simultaneously confirm: (a) prognostic adverse effects of feature_051, feature_011, feature_013, feature_067; (b) modest main effect of feature_039; (c) predictive interactions feature_039:feature_006 (positive), feature_039:feature_092 (positive), feature_039:feature_007 (positive), feature_039:feature_027 (negative), and feature_039:feature_066 (positive).

analyses.code: logit(objective_response ~ feature_039 * (feature_006 + feature_092 + feature_007 + feature_027 + feature_066) + C(feature_051) + feature_011 + feature_067 + feature_013 + C(feature_043) + C(feature_057) + feature_078).
analyses.result_summary: All a priori prognostic effects retained: stage(1) -0.368, stage(2) -0.777, feature_011 -0.039, feature_067 -0.258, feature_013 -0.301 (all p<1e-18). All five interaction effects retained their direction and statistical significance: f039:f006 +0.172 (p=0.001), f039:f092 +0.593 (p=2e-7), f039:f007 +0.141 (p=0.004), f039:f027 -0.205 (p=0.003), f039:f066 +0.032 (p=0.006). Main-effect of feature_039 alone (when biomarkers=0) is now -0.308 (p<1e-5), confirming the treatment helps only when at least one favorable biomarker is present.
